message:
fix(events): EventDeduplicator resets spuriously fall to capacity eviction

Audit of crates/frankenterm-core/src/events.rs for the three concurrency
hazards in the bounded broadcast bus:

  1. await-while-holding-lock: clean. The only `.lock()` sites (record_timestamp,
     oldest_lag_ms on lines 648-668) hold std::sync::Mutex guards across a
     couple of synchronous VecDeque ops and never await.
  2. Drop-on-receiver-close semantics: clean. EventSubscriber::drop
     (lines 784-790) decrements active_subscribers; the broadcast channel
     unsubscribes on Receiver drop.
  3. Unbounded growth under backpressure: clean. EventBus uses fixed-capacity
     broadcast senders; send_routed records a timestamp only on successful
     enqueue and the per-channel times deques are size-capped at `capacity`.

However, EventDeduplicator::check has a real correctness defect in the
window-expired-reset path. When a tracked key's window expires and the
caller checks it again, `check` resets the entry's count / first_seen /
last_seen in place and returns `New` — but does NOT move the key to the
back of `insertion_order`. Capacity evictions pop from the front of
`insertion_order` and use that key to remove the entry from `entries`,
so the just-reset key stays a first-in-line eviction candidate despite
being the most recently active. A small burst of novel keys can evict
it, and the next check of the same key within its reset window then
spuriously returns `New` instead of `Duplicate` — silently failing
the dedup guarantee downstream consumers rely on for notification
cooldown and de-spam suppression.

Fix (events.rs:857-897): on the window-expired-reset branch, also move
the key to the back of `insertion_order` (linear O(n) remove + push_back;
the reset branch is on the cold path). This restores the invariant that
`insertion_order.front()` is always the least-recently-activated key.

Regression test (dedup_window_reset_refreshes_insertion_order_against_
capacity_eviction): with capacity=3, seed a/b/c, let a's window expire,
reset a (New), then add a novel d so capacity fires. After the fix, b
is evicted (oldest live) and a survives — checking a again within the
reset window returns Duplicate{suppressed_count=1}. Before the fix, a
was evicted by d's arrival and the follow-up check returned New.

Also: crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/hybrid_search.rs restores the
`rrf_component_score` helper that 95d5bf42 inadvertently deleted. It's
still used by `rrf_fuse_with_frankensearch` on the `#[cfg(feature =
"frankensearch")]` path and by five unit tests; without it the lib
fails to build under default features.

Verified: `cargo test -p frankenterm-core --no-default-features
--features mcp,asupersync-runtime events::tests::dedup` — 11 passed;
0 failed at /tmp/ft-pane6-target.
